game_description: Buy properties, build houses, and bankrupt your opponents.
filter_1_label: Do you have the most cash?

strategies.strategy_detailed: Orange and red color sets have the highest return on investment — they're landed on most often due to jail proximity. Buy every property you land on in the first lap unless doing so leaves you below $150 in cash.

Railroads scale: 1=$25, 2=$50, 3=$100, 4=$200 per landing. Owning all 4 = 8× single-RR rent — strong without any build cost. But the ceiling caps at $200; you can't develop further to raise it. Buy opportunistically; become a high-priority trade target once you own 3.

strategies.condition: Opponent has one of your color group
strategies.strategy_detailed: Complete your color sets through trading before anyone builds. Offer cash plus a property you don't need. Never give an opponent their monopoly unless you receive one in return — even partial color sets become worthless once you fall behind in building.
strategy_stealth: Never give monopoly without getting one

strategies.condition: Sent to jail in early game
strategies.strategy_detailed: In early game, pay $50 to leave jail immediately — you want to keep landing on and buying properties. Later in the game, stay in jail as long as possible; collecting rent from safety is better than traversing a developed board.
strategy_stealth: Early: pay to leave jail fast
Late: stay in jail — collect safe

strategies.condition: Low on cash after buying properties
strategies.strategy_detailed: Mortgage low-yield properties (utilities, brown set — Mediterranean/Baltic) to fund houses on premium groups. Unmortgaging later costs 110% (10% interest), so plan to rebuild cash first. Freed cash → 3 houses on orange/red, where rent scales 5-7× per house added.

strategies.condition: First to build on a color set
strategies.strategy_detailed: 3 houses per property = best rent-per-dollar (peak marginal ROI). For max house starvation, push to 4 houses on every monopoly — locks more of the 32 tokens, denying opponent builds. NEVER upgrade to hotels: they return 4 houses to the bank, ending your shortage weapon.

Cash advantage means you can offer sweetheart deals that others can't match. Force two opponents to trade with each other for monopolies — then outbuild both. Never let a cash-poor player get a monopoly cheaply; they'll develop slowly while you race ahead.
strategy_stealth: Force opponents to trade each other

strategies.strategy_detailed: Once opponents have hotels, staying in jail for 3 turns is almost always correct. Use the turn in jail to observe board traffic. Pay out only if you need cash from rent you'd miss collecting, or if the board clears enough to move safely.
strategy_stealth: Stay in jail 3 turns on developed board
Pay out only for missed rent

strategies.strategy_detailed: When an opponent is nearly broke, refuse trades that would save them. Accept only trades that give you their monopoly outright for cash at a discount. A struggling player is worth more bankrupt than as a weakened competitor who recovers.

Mortgage everything except your most-landed-on properties. Make targeted deals: give a competitor something that hurts their rival more than it helps them. Survive long enough for opponents to hit each other. One lucky bankruptcy can reset the whole game.
strategy_stealth: Make deals that hurt the leader
Survive until opponents hit each other
